sections.title: Leadership
sections.summary: Martina L. Cheung leads S&P Global as President and CEO, signing the FY2025 10-K on February 10, 2026, with Eric W. Aboaf as CFO since September 2024. Ian P. Livingston chairs a 10-director board, with William Green retiring at the 2026 Annual Meeting.
points: Martina L. Cheung serves as President and CEO and has been a Director since 2024, signing the 10-K on February 10, 2026.
Eric W. Aboaf joined as EVP and CFO with an offer letter dated September 18, 2024, overseeing a company with $15.336 billion in FY2025 revenue.
Christopher F. Craig serves as SVP and Chief Accounting Officer, and Steve Kemps serves as EVP and Chief Legal Officer.

sections.title: Capital Allocation
sections.summary: S&P Global returned $6.2 billion to shareholders in 2025, including $1.2 billion in dividends and $5.0 billion in share repurchases, maintaining over 50 consecutive years of dividend increases. The company ran multiple uncapped accelerated repurchase programs throughout 2025 and authorized a new repurchase program on November 13, 2025.
points: Returned $6.2 billion to shareholders in 2025, split between $1.2 billion in dividends and $5.0 billion in share repurchases.
Maintained more than 50 consecutive years of dividend increases as of 2025, demonstrating consistent capital return discipline.
Initiated uncapped accelerated share repurchase programs on February 19, May 6, August 12, and December 4, 2025, plus a new repurchase authorization on November 13.
Completed divestitures of Engineering Solutions, Leveraged Commentary and Data, PrimeOne, and Fincentric across 2023-2025 to streamline the portfolio.

sections.title: Shareholder Alignment
sections.summary: Approximately 92% of CEO and 82% of other NEOs' 2025 total target annual compensation was performance-based, emphasizing pay-for-performance principles. The company holds 18.4 million securities available for future equity issuance as of December 31, 2025.
points: Approximately 92% of CEO and 82% of other NEOs' 2025 target compensation was performance-based, tying pay to results.
As of December 31, 2025, 982,406 outstanding options had a weighted-average exercise price of $74.46, aligning executive gains with shareholder value.
The board nominated 10 directors for one-year terms at the 2026 Annual Meeting, ensuring annual accountability to shareholders.

sections.title: Communication
sections.summary: S&P Global files detailed 10-K and DEF 14A disclosures with segment-level revenue and adjusted EBITA reporting across five operating segments. The 2026 Proxy Statement reports FY2025 revenue of $15.336 billion up 8% YoY, GAAP net income of $4.471 billion up 16% YoY, and diluted EPS of $14.66 up 19% YoY.
points: The 10-K for FY2025 reports operating segments including Market Intelligence, Ratings, Energy, Mobility, and Indices with a Corporate non-segment category.
The 2026 Proxy Statement discloses segment-level adjusted EBITA margins, including S&P Dow Jones Indices at 71.0% and Mobility at 39.9%.
Ernst & Young LLP audited the consolidated financial statements for the year ended December 31, 2025, with the board recommending ratification for 2026.

sections.title: Governance
sections.summary: Ian P. Livingston chairs a board of 10 nominees with William Green retiring, while the company maintains a five-year revolving credit facility expiring December 17, 2029. Long-term debt of $11.394 billion is balanced against stockholders' equity of $33.159 billion as of FY2025.
points: The board nominated 10 directors for one-year terms at the 2026 Annual Meeting, with William Green not standing for re-election and retiring.
S&P Global holds a five-year revolving credit facility expiring December 17, 2029, and senior notes with maturities ranging from 2026 to 2062, including sustainability-linked notes due 2029.
As of FY2025, the company carried long-term debt of $11,394 million against stockholders' equity of $33,159 million and cash of $1,666 million.

types.summary: S&P Global's intangible assets include databases, software, media content, customer relationships, and trade names, with Goldman Sachs Commodity and Broad Market intellectual property tied to the Indices segment. The company leverages 100+ years of trust and differentiated data and IP across its essential intelligence platform.

The S&P Dow Jones Indices segment generated $1,850 million in FY2025 revenue with a 71.0% adjusted EBITA margin, reflecting how benchmark indices become embedded in investment products and trading strategies. These indices create self-reinforcing adoption as more funds track them, deepening their market authority.

The Market Intelligence segment produced $4,916 million in FY2025 revenue with a 34.0% adjusted EBITA margin, driven by data and analytics integrated into customer workflows. The Mobility segment added $1,747 million in revenue with a 39.9% margin, reflecting embedded relationships with automotive and transportation customers.

S&P Global operates five segments with FY2025 revenue of $15.336 billion and operating cash flow of $3,710 million, leveraging shared data infrastructure and AI-enabled solutions across business lines. The company's scale enables $6.2 billion in annual shareholder returns while investing in high-growth adjacencies and enterprise AI capabilities.
